kicker: ET LEVEL I // READING THE IMPEDANCE PLANE

beats.narration: Every eddy current signal you will ever read lives on one picture: the impedance plane. Your probe is a single bright dot.

payload.title: THE IMPEDANCE PLANE
payload.sub: One map. One moving dot. Every signal.

beats.narration: Resistance runs horizontal, inductive reactance vertical. Couple to metal and the dot lands on the comma-shaped conductivity curve — titanium high, copper low.

payload.heading: Two numbers, one moving dot
payload.note: R HORIZONTAL · XL VERTICAL · DOT RIDES THE CONDUCTIVITY CURVE

beats.narration: Each variable pushes the dot its own way. Lift-off is the biggest, fastest mover, so we rotate it flat — straight toward the air point.
Depth hides in the phase. A deeper flaw answers later and weaker — rotating about fifty-seven degrees per standard depth as its amplitude shrinks.
So read direction first, then size. Phase tells you what it is and how deep; amplitude only tells you how much.

payload.sub: Direction names it. Amplitude only counts it.
